user.title: Connect to ML2MQTT
user.description: Enter the reachable ML2MQTT app URL for model discovery and binding.

data_description.app_url: Use `http://workspace:5000` in the devcontainer setup so Home Assistant can reach the app from inside Docker.

mode.title: Model setup
mode.description: Choose whether to bind an existing model or create a new one from selected Home Assistant entities.

bind_existing.title: Bind existing model
bind_existing.description: Choose an existing ML2MQTT model and the Home Assistant entities that should feed it.

data_description.model_id: Pick an ML2MQTT model that already exists in the app.
data_description.source_entities: Select the Home Assistant entities whose states should be sent to the model when they change.

create_new.title: Create and bind model
create_new.description: Create a new model from Home Assistant entities. ML2MQTT will auto-generate the MQTT topic and helper entities for you.

data_description.model_name: Used for the ML2MQTT model name, helper entities, and the default MQTT topic.
data_description.labels: Enter one label per line or separate them with commas. These become the choices in the Trainer select.
data_description.source_entities: Select the Home Assistant entities whose states should be used as the model inputs.

advanced.name: Advanced options
advanced.description: Optional overrides for the generated MQTT topic and the numeric replacement used when a source entity has no state.

data_description.mqtt_topic: Leave empty to let ML2MQTT generate `ml2mqtt/<model-name>` automatically.
data_description.default_value: Used only when a selected entity reports `unknown`, `unavailable`, or no value.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to the ML2MQTT app API.
error.invalid_labels: Add at least two possible predictions.

abort.already_configured: This ML2MQTT model is already configured.
